Caption: Experimental setup for thermal signature measurement for entropy variation test with the calorimetric method. A) The cell with a temperature sensor (Pt-100) and cables for current and voltage is placed on a 30 mm-thick insulation foam sheet. B) The cell is covered with the same type 30 mm-thick foam sheet with 10 mm aluminum plates on top to act as thermal mass. C) Side view of thermal insulation setup with test cables. Additional pieces of 10 mm-thick foam are added in cracks to reduce thermal leakage. The whole setup is put inside a temperature-controlled chamber.

Schematic of the numerical modeling approach: (a) P2D electrochemical battery model to simulate the electrochemical performance and local heat generation, and (b) 3D finite-element thermal model for prediction of the thermal balance and cell operating temperature.
Variations of equilibrium OCV with the stoichiometric ratio for (a) graphite anode and (b) NMC cathode, obtained from half-cell measurements on coin cells.
Comparison of initial capacities @ C/10 current (a) and dQ/dV plots (b) for the three tested cells at 25 °C compared to all 56 cells tested for initial capacity.
